pages_preview.text_preview: 3/2/25, 1:12 PM SCORE-P2-080: Variable form - replications SCORE-P2-080: Variable form - replications * Indicates required question 1. asana_ticket_name * The name of the asana ticket, used to gather paper ID and RRID information. This should be pre-�lled; please do not edit the text that is in this �eld when you open the form. 2. rr_variables_coder * The �rst and last name of the person �lling out the form. 3. rr_variables_email * The email address of the person �lling out the form. 4. rr_labteam_contributor * The name(s) of the author(s) of the report, separated by commas. 5. rr_num_claims_report * The number of unique claims being evaluated in this replication attempt. https://docs.google.com/forms/d/1gJpOeRdHZImOSfIiTvYC4ADaR_G92rGa2LXC85Tlau8/edit?no_redirect=true&pli=1 1/13

3/2/25, 1:12 PM SCORE-P2-080: Variable form - replications 6. rr_num_outcomes_report * The number of outcomes being reported in this form response, i.e., the number of lines expected in each response below. This should be the same as the number of claims *except* for single-trace Direct Replication projects that conducted two rounds of sampling. For each item in the rest of this form, enter one answer per line in the text box. Make sure that (a) all analyses are included and (b) they are entered in the same order for all answers. Do not leave lines blank; if an outcome has no answer, the line should contain only 'na' . In other words, there should be the same number of lines in each text box. The easiest way to prepare your responses to this form is to draft them in a google sheet with a column for each item in the form, and then copy each column into the form. 7. c4_claim_id * What is the claim_id for the claim 4 that is the reference from the original paper for the analysis? For tests based on a single-trace claim, just write 'single-trace'. 8. rr_original_data_overlap * Is the underlying data for the analysis completely new, (approximately) equal to the original study, or a combination of new and original data? Provide one per line: new, original, hybrid https://docs.google.com/forms/d/1gJpOeRdHZImOSfIiTvYC4ADaR_G92rGa2LXC85Tlau8/edit?no_redirect=true&pli=1 2/13

3/2/25, 1:12 PM SCORE-P2-080: Variable form - replications 9. rr_analytic_sample_stage * What was the target sample stage for the analysis? Provide one per line: stage 1, stage 2, threshold, no target 10. rr_analytic_sample_size_value_reported * What is the �nal analytic sample size? This is just in terms of the unit of analysis that was used for the power analysis, if one was done; there will be another space below to provide more information about other levels of observations if there are any. 11. rr_analytic_sample_size_units_reported * What is the unit for the sample size reported above, e.g., students, participants, countries, etc.? https://docs.google.com/forms/d/1gJpOeRdHZImOSfIiTvYC4ADaR_G92rGa2LXC85Tlau8/edit?no_redirect=true&pli=1 3/13

3/2/25, 1:12 PM SCORE-P2-080: Variable form - replications 12. rr_expected_sample_reached_reported * Did the analytic sample size meet the target sample size? Provide one per line: yes, no, no target 13. rr_analytic_sample_cells_reported * Provide a description of the hierarchical/multi-level structure of the data, or, in the case of a factorial design, a description of the group sample sizes across levels of the independent variable(s). 14. rr_statistic_fulltext_reported * Provide the full text report of the result of the given statistical test, something like "F(3,59) = 0.63" or "b = 0.78, SE = 0.125". The following items will break this text into its component parts. In the case of non-inferential evidence, write 'na'. https://docs.google.com/forms/d/1gJpOeRdHZImOSfIiTvYC4ADaR_G92rGa2LXC85Tlau8/edit?no_redirect=true&pli=1 4/13

3/2/25, 1:12 PM SCORE-P2-080: Variable form - replications 15. rr_statistic_analysis_type_reported * What type of analysis is being reported? Provide one per line: Sample mean comparison; ANOVA/ANCOVA; Logistic regression; Single equation regression; Multilevel regression; Other regression; Bivariate/multivariate correlation; Contingency table; Other non- parametric test; Structural equation model; Comparison between two correlations or coe�cients; Meta-analysis; Non-inferential evidence 16. rr_statistic_interaction_reported * Is the effect an interaction or not? Provide one per line: interaction, main or simple effect, not sure The next few items are focused on regression analyses, so it is expected that not all analyses may have a response. However, please continue to include 'na' for all such analyses to maintain the same number of lines across all of the items in the form. 17. rr_coefficient_type_reported * Is the reported coe�cient standardized or unstandardized? Provide one per line: standardized, unstandardized https://docs.google.com/forms/d/1gJpOeRdHZImOSfIiTvYC4ADaR_G92rGa2LXC85Tlau8/edit?no_redirect=true&pli=1 5/13

3/2/25, 1:12 PM SCORE-P2-080: Variable form - replications 18. rr_coefficient_value_reported * What is the value of the regression coe�cient? 19. rr_coefficient_se_reported * What is the value of the standard error of that coe�cient? 20. rr_total_model_parameters * How many predictors are in the full regression model? Please include the intercept, all controls/covariates, interactions, lower order terms, etc. https://docs.google.com/forms/d/1gJpOeRdHZImOSfIiTvYC4ADaR_G92rGa2LXC85Tlau8/edit?no_redirect=true&pli=1 6/13
